title: Podman Mac M3 running x86 containers emits dozens of core files
body:
### Issue Description

Describe your issue

Podman Mac M3 running x86 containers emits dozens of sftp core files.  It does so into the bindmount, sullying my beautiful host filesystem.

Is there a way to quiet them?


```
file qemu_sftp_20240405-164217_3634.core
qemu_sftp_20240405-164217_3634.core: ELF 64-bit LSB core file, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), SVR4-style, from '/usr/bin/sftp -b - -vvv -C -o ControlMaster=auto -o ControlPersist=60s -o Stric', real uid: 0, effective uid: 0, real gid: 0, effective gid: 0, execfn: '/usr/bin/sftp', platform: 'x86_64'
```

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.1
API Version:  5.0.1
Go Version:   go1.22.1
Git Commit:   946d055df324e4ed6c1e806b561af4740db4fea9
Built:        Mon Apr  1 08:28:31 2024
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.0.1
API Version:  5.0.1
Go Version:   go1.21.8
Built:        Sun Mar 31 20:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.  Run an x86 image on an arm/m3 mac
```
podman run \
  --interactive \
  --tty \
  --privileged \
  --rm \
  --name=agnosticd \
  --volume=${PWD}:/runner/agnosticd \
  --workdir=/runner/agnosticd \
  quay.io/agnosticd/ee-multicloud:v0.1.2-rc
```
3.
4.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

I did not expect hundreds of qemu-sftp core files.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Same issue with an M2 Pro

```
> podman run --rm -it --arch=amd64 docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest

root@b05ecb91a182:/# uname -a
Linux b05ecb91a182 6.8.8-300.fc40.aarch64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat Apr 27 18:11:03 UTC 2024 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
root@b05ecb91a182:/# dpkg -l | perl -ne 'print if /grep/'
ii  grep                    3.11-4build1           amd64        GNU grep, egrep and fgrep
root@b05ecb91a182:/#
root@b05ecb91a182:/# grep
qemu-x86_64-static: QEMU internal SIGSEGV {code=MAPERR, addr=0x20}
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
```

CPU: Apple M2 Pro
Version: Ventura 13.5.2

Hope it helps

These systems are using qemu-user-static on Linux which has no guarantees on how well it handles emulation, obviously in this case, it is not handling it well.  We should be coming out with Rosetta support, which might work better.
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21670
Can confirm that with podman v5.1.0 (which includes Rosetta support) the issue is gone.

local podman (macos)
```
❯ podman -v
podman version 5.1.1
```

podman on the newly upgraded podman machine
```
~
❯ podman machine ssh "podman -v"
podman version 5.1.1
```

Still an error

Emulation is somewhat limited as they have to emulate the possible cpu instructions. If there are missing some the program just crashes.

If something doesn't run with rosetta you are better of reporting that to apple.
But the same for user qemu-user-static you have to report there.
Podman cannot fix this things so I close this one.

Has this been fixed , on Podman 5.2 I'm seeing same issue at M1 Ventura 13.2.1
`rosetta error: unhandled auxillary vector type 28`

> Has this been fixed , on Podman 5.2 I'm seeing same issue at M1 Ventura 13.2.1 `rosetta error: unhandled auxillary vector type 28`

same error here trying to run an x86 image using podman
this error does not happen using docker

> Has this been fixed , on Podman 5.2 I'm seeing same issue at M1 Ventura 13.2.1 `rosetta error: unhandled auxillary vector type 28`

I fixed my issue updating my M1 from 13 to 14 Sonoma.

> > Has this been fixed , on Podman 5.2 I'm seeing same issue at M1 Ventura 13.2.1 `rosetta error: unhandled auxillary vector type 28`
> 
> I fixed my issue updating my M1 from 13 to 14 Sonoma.

Thanks for the quick response and the solution! I also saw [here](https://github.com/abiosoft/colima/issues/926#issuecomment-1911262852) that upgrading macos might fix this. Will try, ty!
